key_matchups: Messi vs Upamecano (Upamecano wins an aerial duel on Messi)
Romero vs Upamecano (Upamecano barges into Romero in the box)
De Paul vs Rabiot (early physical duel after De Paul's shove)
phases.summary: Argentina start with high tempo, linking Messi and Di Maria to threaten from the left and through the D. Upamecano's early duel with Messi and the Romero-Upamecano collision highlight the opening aerial/physical battles. France respond with patient midfield build-up and a compact defense, closing ranks as Argentina press, while Mbappe begins to look to involve himself with a left-sided foray and a one-two with Rabiot.

Argentina take the lead via Messi's 23rd-minute penalty after a Di Maria run draws contact from Dembele, and extend it to 2-0 when Di Maria finishes a move at 36'. France push back with set-piece threats and aerial duels as Giroud challenges Romero and a late duel between Hernandez and Fernandez unfolds.

adjustments: France substitutions: Dembele and Giroud off; Thuram and Kolo Muani on.
Argentina substitution: Lautaro Martinez introduced in extra time.
phases.summary: France trail 2-0 at half-time and respond with a double substitution to inject pace and a focal attacker as Thuram and Muani replace Dembele and Giroud. Argentina hold the lead and push into extra time, with Lautaro Martinez’s introduction providing a timely boost.

adjustments: Argentina sit back in a low block
France implement a double substitution; subsequently enjoy more possession
key_matchups: Thuram (France) vs Argentina's back line (left flank)
Fernandez (Argentina) vs Kolo Muani (France) – booking after clattering Muani
Muani (France) vs Otamendi (Argentina) – Muani wins ball and feeds Mbappe
phases.summary: Argentina sit deep in a low block and press with intensity, stifling France’s buildup. After France’s halftime double change they enjoy more possession but still struggle to convert, as Argentina’s shape remains compact and dangerous on the break. The phase features key duels—Griezmann vs Fernandez and Mbappe/Muani against Otamendi—that define the tempo and momentum of the period.

adjustments: France substitutions: Kolo Muani and Thuram introduced, providing fresh attacking options and momentum.
Camavinga pressing De Paul to disrupt Argentina's midfield build-up.
phases.summary: Mbappe's 80' penalty put France ahead, and his 81' goal made it 2-2; Messi then struck at 109' to put Argentina 3-2 ahead, before Mbappe's 118' penalty rescued a 3-3 draw. The phase features substitution-driven momentum shifts for France and high-intensity duels (Camavinga-De Paul, Muani-Otamendi, Thuram-Fernandez) amid late drama.
